claim: Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over at the May 7, 2026 meeting, with a split vote.

provenance.definition: Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over at the May 7, 2026 meeting, with a split vote.

cases.description: Banxico ends easing cycle with 3-2 cut to 6.50% on May 7, 2026.
cases.basis_quote: The central bank declared an end to its two-year easing cycle.

claim: The 3-2 split at the May 2026 meeting indicates meaningful internal disagreement on the board.

cases.description: May 7, 2026 Banxico vote was 3-2, with Heath and Espinosa dissenting.
cases.basis_quote: Board members Jonathan Heath and Irene Espinosa Cantellano dissented, preferring to hold the rate at 6.75%.

claim: Mexico's economy contracted and inflation forecast for Q2 2026 was revised upward to 4.1%.
